summary: coral-wraith postinstall harvests AWS credentials and npmrc tokens, exfiltrates to hardcoded remote IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a shell pipeline that (1) reads `/root/.npmrc`, `/home/node/.npmrc`, and `/app/.npmrc`, (2) exports `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID` / `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` / `AWS_SESSION_TOKEN` and runs `aws sts get-caller-identity` plus `aws secretsmanager list-secrets` / `get-secret-value` across regions, (3) installs `boto3` via `python3 -m pip install boto3 -q` and runs an inline `python3 -c` harvester against AWS Secrets Manager, and (4) scans the filesystem for HTB flags and YAML credential stores. The collected output is base64-encoded and PUT to `http://154.57.164.71:30782/api/modules/ECT-839201` (with `http://127.0.0.1:30782` as a fallback target list entry). Execution is gated to skip when the npm registry matches Chinese mirrors (`npmmirror|tencent|nijin|hscan|ustc|huawei|cnpm|taobao`) or when the cwd is under `/tmp/`, indicating deliberate evasion of automated supply-chain scanners. The hardcoded bare-IP exfiltration endpoint, AWS credential harvesting via lifecycle hook, and scanner-evasion gating jointly establish hostile intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
